name: the-presidents-club
description: Nancy Gibbs and Michael Duffy's The Presidents Club — presidential history toolkit exploring the secret relationships among modern US presidents after they leave office, from Truman-Hoover to Bush-Clinton to Obama's learning curve
useCase: Understand the hidden network of former US presidents, learn from their relationships, and apply the club's wisdom to leadership transitions
knownRisks: The book focuses heavily on personality and anecdote rather than systematic analysis
Some claims (especially about Nixon) are disputed by partisans on both sides
The club's relevance diminishes with fewer living former presidents
